title: Quarterly Asset Review
description: Build a quarterly review for a property or small portfolio covering performance trends, risk shifts, leasing outlook, capex priorities, and recommended strategic actions.
whyItMatters: Quarterly reviews are where property management graduates into advisory work. They ask the manager to move beyond monthly updates and show whether the asset is strengthening, drifting, or approaching a decision point that ownership should address.
skillsLearned: Trend analysis across occupancy, NOI, cost, and service

steps.title: Compile the quarter-to-date picture
steps.description: Summarize occupancy movement, leasing outlook, collections, budget performance, major works, incidents, contractor performance, and tenant sentiment over the full quarter.
steps.tips: Quarterly reviews should show trend, not just three monthly snapshots stuck together.

steps.title: Identify the critical themes
steps.description: Choose the 3-5 themes that best explain current asset position such as stabilizing occupancy, aging plant risk, stronger collections, tenant-service improvement, or ESG opportunity.
steps.tips: A useful review is selective. It should not feel like every available report pasted into one deck.

steps.title: Frame decisions and scenarios
steps.description: Recommend actions on leasing strategy, capex priorities, operating adjustments, vendor changes, or risk mitigation. Where useful, show a best-case and downside scenario.
steps.tips: Advisory value comes from making choices legible before the owner asks for them.

steps.title: Prepare the executive pack
steps.description: Write a concise meeting pack with charts, key messages, and a decision page that could be presented in a 30-minute review meeting.
steps.tips: If the decision page is weak, the whole review will feel descriptive rather than strategic.

deliverables: A quarterly review pack or deck
A trend summary across core KPIs
A strategic action list with priority and owner support needed
A decision page for the review meeting

rubric.description: The review demonstrates real asset thinking rather than only operational reporting.
Performance movement is explained clearly with meaningful causes and implications.
Actions are specific, commercially sensible, and tied to the evidence presented.
The final pack is concise and credible enough for a client or senior leadership meeting.
